commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading (#217)

* ⚡ Bolt: Optimize PythonJsonWorkerPool stderr failure reading

The prior code used `ReadToEndAsync` to drain `stderr` when a Python worker process failed to return a valid response envelope. While this was wrapped in a cancellation token, `ReadToEndAsync` only cancels at internal read boundaries, meaning a process dumping megabytes of Python tracebacks would still cause large allocations and hang the application while the OS pipe buffer slowly flushed.

This change replaces `ReadToEndAsync` with a `ReadBoundedStderrAsync` method, which:
- Reads a maximum of 50 lines or 4096 characters using `ReadLineAsync` with strict `WaitAsync` boundaries.
- Silently drains the remainder of the pipe, allowing the OS pipe buffer to clear and ensuring the underlying subprocess can fully exit.
- Interpolates the previously unused `killSuffix` variable to accurately report when a kill operation was attempted before reading `stderr`.

This fix prevents hangs and OOM spikes when Python models crash.

Co-authored-by: mta-babel <32500316+mta-babel@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove temporary-exception entitlement rejected by App Store (#578)

## Problem
Apple rejected the Mac App Store submission under **Guideline 2.4.5(i) -
Performance**:

> Entitlement
`com.apple.security.temporary-exception.files.home-relative-path.read-only`
value "/" is not permitted.

Temporary exception entitlements are not approved for new Mac App Store
submissions.

## Fix
Removed the
`com.apple.security.temporary-exception.files.home-relative-path.read-write`
entitlement from `Entitlements.AppStore.plist`.

**This entitlement was unnecessary** because:
- The macOS sandbox remaps `HOME` to
`~/Library/Containers/<bundle-id>/Data/`
- All `UserProfile`-based paths (`~/.polypilot/`, `~/.copilot/`) resolve
inside the container automatically
- The copilot helper process inherits the sandbox (via
`Entitlements.Helper.plist`) and sees the same remapped `HOME`

## Testing
- All 3331 unit tests pass
- No code changes needed — only the entitlements plist was modified

commit.message:
fix(generator): align = operators in WHERE clause conditions (#49)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Aligns `=` operators in `WHERE` clause conditions so equality
comparisons are easy to scan vertically.

## Root Cause

`where_sql` delegated to `_inline_clause_sql`, which only handles the
leading-`AND` layout. It rendered each condition with natural spacing:

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (
    p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key
    OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL
  )
```

## Fix

`where_sql` now works at the AST level when the top-level is a pure
`AND` chain:

1. Flattens the `AND` chain with `_flatten_and`.
2. Collects all `EQ` nodes anywhere in the `WHERE` tree and computes
`max LHS width + 2` as the alignment target.
3. Pads top-level equality LHS to the target column.
4. Renders top-level `Paren` conditions compactly (single line) via
`_compact_sql`.

```sql
WHERE p.time_frame            = _time_frame
  AND e.participant_key       = _participant_key
  AND e.enabled
  AND (p.program_supplier_key = _program_supplier_key OR _program_supplier_key IS NULL)
```

Falls back to `_inline_clause_sql` when the top-level condition is not
`AND` (e.g. a bare `OR`), preserving existing connector alignment
behaviour.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new `where_sql`, `_flatten_and`,
`_compact_sql`
- `tests/fixtures/select/where_eq_alignment.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture pair
- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/create_macro.expected.sql` — regenerated to
reflect aligned three-EQ WHERE
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — new "WHERE — `=` operator column
alignment" section

Resolves #48

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add offline docs.rs-style workspace docs check to Rust CI and fix surfaced rustdoc links (#1295)

Rust CI only validated docs for a single crate, which allowed docs.rs
regressions elsewhere in the workspace. This PR adds a workspace-wide
docs.rs simulation in offline mode so doc breakage is caught before
merge.

- **CI coverage: docs.rs simulation for the full Rust workspace**
- Added `rust::ci-docs-rs` to run a docs.rs-like build across all
workspace crates.
- Uses `DOCS_RS=1`, `--cfg docsrs`, nightly rustdoc, and offline doc
generation.

- **CI integration**
- Wired `ci-docs-rs` into `rust::ci-lint` so the check runs on standard
Rust CI paths.

- **Rustdoc correctness fixes uncovered by workspace-wide docs**
- Fixed broken/private intra-doc links across `mlt-core`, `mlt`,
`mlt-synthetics`, and `mlt-wasm`.
- Replaced invalid links to private or out-of-scope symbols with valid
public links or plain text where appropriate.

```just
ci-docs-rs:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    rustup toolchain install nightly --profile minimal
    export DOCS_RS=1
    export RUSTDOCFLAGS="${RUSTDOCFLAGS:+$RUSTDOCFLAGS } --cfg docsrs"
    cargo fetch --locked
    CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true cargo +nightly doc --workspace --no-deps --locked
```

fix: strip Report-To, NEL, COOP, COEP, CORP response headers

These headers were forwarded to the browser unchanged, causing two problems:

1. Report-To + NEL: instruct the browser to send network error/timing reports
   directly to upstream endpoints, leaking real hostnames and client traffic
   details even when the proxy successfully masks them from page content.

2. Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy (same-origin) + Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy
   (require-corp) enforce strict cross-origin isolation. In a proxy context
   this breaks subresource loading: COEP blocks resources that lack the
   correct CORP opt-in header, and COOP prevents cross-window communication
   across the proxied origin boundary.

3. Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy (same-origin) blocks the proxy from serving
   cross-origin subresource responses to the browser.

Add all six variants to headersStrip. Extend TestProxyCSPHSTSStripped to
assert all new headers are absent from proxy responses.

commit.message:
fix: deflate oversized body restoration was broken — remove inner LimitReader

When maxBodyBytes was set (e.g. -max-body 1), the deflate code was doing
io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxBodyBytes+1)), which read only a
partial compressed stream. The early-exit then restored those partial bytes
back to resp.Body, causing the client to receive truncated compressed data.

Fix: read the ENTIRE compressed body upfront (no inner size limit on the
compressed stream). The decompressed-size limit at the shared oversized-body
check (maxBodyBytes gate below) handles the limit correctly. When the
decompressed body exceeds maxBodyBytes, the oversized branch now restores
the complete compressedDeflate bytes and the Content-Encoding header.

Add TestDeflateOversizedBodyRestored to verify the fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix HTTP server failing to bind net_port

Sv_InitHttp runs during Sv_Init, before any map load triggers
Net_Config which creates the net_port cvar. Use Cvar_Add instead
of Cvar_Get so the cvar is created if it doesn't exist yet.

commit.message:
Merge User-Agent headers when cloning HTTP client (#383)

When `Client.clone()` was called with headers containing a `User-Agent`,
it would overwrite the base client's `User-Agent` rather than merging.
This loses the SDK's default UA token when callers supply their own.

## Changes

- **`packages/common/src/microsoft_teams/common/http/client.py`**
- Added `_merge_headers(base, overrides)` helper: performs a
case-insensitive `User-Agent` merge (space-concatenated, token-based
dedup to avoid substring false positives); all other headers follow
standard last-write-wins semantics
- `Client.clone()` now calls `_merge_headers()` instead of a plain dict
unpack

- **`packages/common/tests/test_client.py`**
- Added 7 tests covering: preservation without overrides, merge when
both sides define UA, non-UA overrides leave UA untouched,
case-insensitive key matching, duplicate-token suppression, substring
false-positive guard, and override-only UA when base has none

## Example

```python
client = Client(ClientOptions(headers={"User-Agent": "teams-py/1.0"}))
clone = client.clone(ClientOptions(headers={"User-Agent": "myapp/2.0"}))

# Before: "myapp/2.0"  (SDK UA lost)
# After:  "teams-py/1.0 myapp/2.0"
print(clone._options.headers["User-Agent"])
```

fix: deflate oversized-body corruption + add deflate to Accept-Encoding

Two related fixes to the deflate Content-Encoding support:

1. Oversized deflate body was corrupted on the round-trip back.
   When the decompressed deflate body exceeded maxBodyBytes, the code fell
   into the 'else' branch which called io.MultiReader(bytes.NewReader(raw),
   resp.Body), but resp.Body was already empty (consumed for zlib/flate
   detection) and 'raw' held decompressed bytes with Content-Encoding
   already stripped. The browser would receive partial decompressed data
   with no Content-Encoding — completely broken.

   Fix: hoist compressedDeflate to outer scope; in the oversized branch,
   restore resp.Body = bytes.NewReader(compressedDeflate) and put back
   Content-Encoding: deflate so the browser receives the original stream.

   Also added a short-circuit: if the compressed body itself exceeds
   maxBodyBytes, skip immediately rather than decompressing first.

2. Accept-Encoding: gzip, identity → gzip, deflate, identity
   Now that deflate is supported, advertise it so servers can use it
   (instead of only gzip) when serving to the proxy.

commit.message:
Fix context menus rendering behind floating sidebar toolbar (#580)

## Problem
Context menus (session right-click, group `⋯` menu, header `⋯` overflow)
render **behind** the floating sidebar toolbar buttons (New, Manual
sort, refresh, filter chips).

## Root Cause
Multiple positioned elements with explicit `z-index` values create
**stacking contexts** that trap descendant `position: fixed` menus —
even though the menus have `z-index: 10000`, they can't escape a parent
stacking context with a lower z-index.

## Fix
- **Session/group context menus:** Add `has-open-menu` CSS class to
`sidebar-content` when any menu is open. Collapse stacking contexts on
toolbar elements (`sidebar-top-tools`, `sidebar-compose-panel`,
`sidebar-organize-shell-compact`, `sidebar-support-fab`) to `z-index:
auto` — buttons stay visible but the menu paints on top.
- **Header overflow menu:** Use `:has()` to bump `.sidebar-header` from
z-index 3 → 50 when the overflow is hovered/focused.
- **Cleanup:** Remove unnecessary `z-index: 2` from `.session-actions`.

commit.message:
feat: add deflate Content-Encoding decompression support

When an upstream sends Content-Encoding: deflate (despite our Accept-Encoding
request for gzip/identity), the proxy now decompresses the body, applies string
replacements, and strips the Content-Encoding header — same as for gzip.

HTTP 'deflate' is ambiguous: some servers send zlib-wrapped DEFLATE (RFC 1950,
0x78 magic byte) while others send raw DEFLATE (RFC 1951). Try zlib first;
fall back to raw flate if the first two bytes aren't the zlib header (0x78 xx).

Also fix: CSP ws:// scheme was incorrectly mapped to http:// instead of ws://
(ws:// fell through to the switch default). Now both wss:// and ws:// map to
ws:// correctly.

Tests added:
- TestDeflateResponseBodyRewrite: zlib-wrapped + raw-deflate subtests
- CSP ws-scheme test case already committed separately

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: campaigns trigger on score decline, not just rejection streaks

Campaign triggers are now Gate rules:
  reliabilityCampaignRule = Gate.or(consecutiveRejects >= N, reliabilityDeclining(2))
  modularityCampaignRule  = Gate.or(consecutiveRejects >= N, modularityDeclining(2))
  radicalPlanRule         = consecutiveRejects >= N (only on streaks)

Each campaign fires independently when its rule returns SUCCESS:
- Reliability declining 2+ iterations → reliability campaign
- Modularity declining 2+ iterations → modularity campaign
- Both declining → both campaigns run
- Rejection streak → all three (campaigns + radical plan)

This catches divergence BEFORE the terminator hits Rule 3 (stop).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: soften parchment theme for easier reading

The parchment (light) theme had near-black text (#1e1408) on warm beige,
creating a harsh 14.8:1 contrast ratio. Softened the entire palette:
- text: #1e1408 → #44392a (10.1:1, comfortable)
- accent: #7a3d12 → #8a5025 (warmer, 5.8:1)
- muted: #8a7060 → #7d6b58 (now meets WCAG AA at 4.6:1)
- bg/surface/border: lighter, less saturated yellow

Also fixed undefined --text-muted variable → --muted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: remove dead fallback code in replacer; add case-insensitive tests

The loop-based fallbacks in ToOriginal/ToAlias/ToOriginalDiff/ToAliasDiff
were dead code: r.reReq/reResp are nil iff there are no pairs, so the loops
would never iterate anyway. Remove the loops and the helpers they relied on
(replaceCI, countOccurrences, max).

Also add three tests for the production-default case-insensitive mode:
- TestCaseInsensitiveReplacer: ToAlias/ToOriginal match mixed case
- TestCaseSensitiveReplacer: -cs mode rejects wrong case
- TestCaseInsensitiveDiff: ToAliasDiff counts CI replacements correctly
